reasoning: The stock (INTU) suffered a significant drop in January 2026, falling from ~650 to 497.38 on Friday, Jan 30 (which will be the reference price for Feb 1). This decline was driven by analyst downgrades and fears of AI disruption. However, even the downgraded analyst targets (e.g. $700) are far above the current price of $497. The stock is currently hitting long-term support levels and is technically heavily oversold. The statistical trend analysis gives an estimated 61% probability of being higher than 497.4 next week due to mean reversion. We estimate a 60% chance of a short-term bounce by Feb 8th. The second resolution date (Mar 3) falls after the expected Q2 earnings report on Feb 26. Because earnings introduce much more variance and uncertainty as to the stock's longer-term trajectory, and adhering to the instruction that uncertainty should increase with horizon, the March probability is pulled closer to 0.5.
